title: Open Ethics Compliance (OEC)
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"29i6v","text":"Open Ethics Compliance Database (OECDb)¹ is an open source archive of the online most respected
organizations, policy charters, compliances, researches, heritages,
opportunities, and tools concerning the implementation of open
projects. The purpose of this invention disclosure is to allow for
its priceless open knowledge commons to be researched, safeguarded,
and promoted as the open heritage of humanity it is. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":39},{"style":"bold","offset":40,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":40,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9lug7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ifiv","text":"Open Ethics International Compliance (OEC)² is the open science methodology I used to
develop OEIDb through years of collaborative researches with the most
respected institutions along communities worldwide. It was catalogued
for the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) and made
public with the Quaker United Nations Office (QUNO) delegation in the
Common Good Organization (CoGO) Congress during the United Nations
Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD) eWeek World Summit on
the Information Society (WSIS). 
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d2pgs","text":"1. doi.org/10.7910/DVN/CVX1ZL.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":3,"length":26}],"data":{}},{"key":"3h50g","text":"2. doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16946157.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":3,"length":31}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B4B0AFE7828811F0A1F40A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/CVX1ZL"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"8CCF9BC5828811F0A1F40A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16946157"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ug9p","text":"Follow each step thoroughly under the public international law (PIL), local rule of law (RoL), and the scientific methodology.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Rule of Law Open Science (RoLOS) by Felipe Ribeiro
Nonviolent Nonprofit Open Education License (NNOEL) by Felipe Ribeiro
'Open Ethics Compliance Database (OECDb)' Curatorship Open Ethics Compliance Database (OECDb)
Choose all the organizations in the 'Organizations' folder that concern your project's area of action.
Comply to your chosen organizations' charters present in the 'Charters' folder.
Apply to your chosen organizations' required policy charters regulatory compliances in the 'Compliances' folder to acquire their due institutional accreditation and individual certifications.
Curatorship Open Ethics Principles and Rules Decision https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curatorship
Study your chosen organizations' researches, heritage, and courses in the 'Researches', 'Heritages', and 'Opportunities' folders.
Transition your project's developments to the software and tools in the 'Tools' folder as much as possible to facilitate interoperability with your chosen organizations.
This procedure shall assist your project in the fundraising the grants with the sponsors in the 'Opportunities' folder.
creator: Felipe Ribeiro
